records.ety: [F. intention, L. intentio. See Intend, and cf. Intension.]

defs: A stretching or bending of the mind toward an object; closeness of application; fixedness of attention; earnestness.
quotes: Intention is when the mind, with great earnestness, and of choice, fixes its view on any idea.

defs: A determination to act in a certain way or to do a certain thing; purpose; design; as, an intention to go to New York.
quotes: Hell is paved with good intentions.

defs: The object toward which the thoughts are directed; end; aim.
quotes: In [chronical distempers], the principal intention is to restore the tone of the solid parts.

defs: The state of being strained. See Intension.
Any mental apprehension of an object.
